title: Section 10
content: For the purposes of this Act, any document purporting to be signed by a judge or officer of a Court outside 1 [India] shall, until the contrary is proved, be deemed to have been so signed without proof of the signature of judicial or official character of the person appearing to have signed it, and the officerof a Court by whom a document is signed shall, until the contrary is provided, be deemed to have been the proper officer of the Court to sign the document.
footnote: 1. Subs. by Act 3 of 1951, s. 3 and the Schedule, for "the States".
